name: Louisiana Creole

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Louisiana Creole French (loui1240).

notes: Skeleton. French-lexifier creole of Louisiana written in a Latin, French-based spelling. Grapheme map follows French conventions adapted to the creole: ou [u], ch [ʃ], j [ʒ], gn [ɲ], the nasal-vowel digraphs an/en [ã], on [ɔ̃], in [ɛ̃], and accented è/é. Orthographic practice varies between writers and etymological French spellings; final silent letters, liaison and any prosody are not reliably recoverable and are left unencoded, so a PER floor is expected.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Louisiana Creole French (loui1240)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
